target.name: Genesis AI
target.founder: Zhou Xian, Theophile Gervet, Tsun-Hsuan Wang

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $105M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Genesis AI, a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zhou Xian, Theophile Gervet, and Tsun-Hsuan Wang, has raised $105M to date. The valuation universe includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers in robotics and manufacturing, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Stage-progression methodology is the sole applicable triangulation approach. Using the $105M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier yields an implied valuation of $262M. Peer-median analysis was not executable; while seven same-sector peers exist, fewer than three datapoints survived filtering for stage and valuation tier proximity, making broad sector medians unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $223M (low) to $302M (high), with a median point estimate of $262M. This range reflects the stage-progression step-up alone and carries material uncertainty. Key limitations include reliance on a single method and absence of narrow-set comparable transaction or revenue multiples. Validation of this range requires either (1) identification of sufficiently similar-stage, robotics-focused private valuations, or (2) revenue or EBITDA data to ground bottom-up multiples. Until such data surfaces, confidence in this valuation should be treated as preliminary.
